summary: Frontend-only PWA regression test for AbsensiPro. All 4 review-request scenarios PASS. Service worker is correctly registered and activated, but does NOT interfere with /api auth calls or dashboard stats. App loads, owner can log in, and dashboard renders normally with full nav (9 items) and real stats values.

issues: Tailwind CDN still in use (cdn.tailwindcss.com console warning) — pre-existing, not a PWA regression.

critical_code_review_comments: sw.js is implemented safely: skips non-GET, /api, and cross-origin; navigations are network-first with cached /index.html fallback; static GETs use stale-while-revalidate. Good.
manifest.json is valid with name, short_name, start_url, scope, display=standalone, theme_color, lang=id, and 192/512/maskable icons.
index.html includes <link rel="manifest">, apple-touch-icon, theme-color, mobile-web-app-capable, and registers /sw.js on window.load. Good.
Minor: SW only caches a small fixed shell (no precaching of /modules/*.js or fonts/CDN). That is intentional and safe — keeps API/dynamic logic fresh. Acceptable.
Pre-existing: Tailwind Play CDN warning remains (not PWA-related).
context_for_next_testing_agent: PWA verification details: GET /manifest.json -> 200 JSON name='AbsensiPro — Sistem Absensi Karyawan', icons 192/512/maskable-512 present. GET /sw.js -> 200 application/javascript (1593 bytes). GET /icons/icon-192.png -> 200 image/png (20919 bytes). GET /icons/icon-512.png -> 200 image/png (169917 bytes). index.html contains <link rel=manifest>, apple-touch-icon, theme-color, and serviceWorker registration script. In-browser: navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistration() returned active+'activated' with scope=site root. After SW activated, owner login (owner@company.com/password123) succeeded and routed to /#dashboard. Sidebar nav-* items present: dashboard, checkin, history, monitoring, employees, approvals, offices, face, profile. Dashboard rendered real values (Total Karyawan=1, Tidak Hadir=1, weekly chart, Per Departemen Direksi 0/1). No console errors except the unrelated Tailwind CDN warning. A direct fetch test from page context to /api/dashboard/stats returned 401 because the test used a wrong storage key for the token — this is a test-script artifact, not an app bug; the live dashboard's own fetch with correct auth populated stats successfully.
rca of the issue: No issue found. PWA changes are non-regressive.
